page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-48 is a general-purpose server powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a base speed of 2.3 GHz. The instance is configured with 180.0 GB of RAM, translating to 3.75 GB per core. It does not include bundled local storage or integrated GPUs, and has zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The underlying x86_64 architecture supports advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE and AMX_INT8). These hardware accelerators contribute to strong performance in specialized tasks. In benchmarks, the server achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 2175.0 and a multi-core score of 18084.0. Passmark CPU tests yield a score of 54598.51, with strong results in extended instructions and encryption.

Memory subsystem performance is characterized by a strong cached read bandwidth of 3389.0 GB/sec, though uncached large-block reads and writes drop to average levels of 144.84 GB/sec and 61.98 GB/sec respectively. The server demonstrates strong throughput in static web serving at 74.96 GB/sec and Redis SET operations at 60.15 million operations per second. For machine learning workloads, the CPU-based LLM inference speeds are notable, processing 3296.88 tokens per second for a small 135M model and 24.25 tokens per second for a large 70B model.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c4-standard-48 offers a balanced resource density suitable for enterprise workloads without the premium cost of dedicated GPU hardware. However, the lack of local NVMe storage means all storage operations must rely on network-attached volumes, which represents a potential I/O bottleneck. This server is well-suited for software compilation, image processing, database operations, web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: The Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-48 is an x86_64 general-purpose server configured with 48 dedicated vCPUs (24 physical cores) powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor running at 2.3 GHz, paired with 180.0 GB of RAM. It features no local storage or GPUs. The processor includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX, enabling top-tier performance in image processing and single-core Geekbench tests. Benchmarks show strong multi-core capabilities, with a Passmark CPU score of 54598.51 and a static web serving throughput of 74.96 GB/sec. Memory performance is robust, delivering 3389.0 GB/sec for cached reads. This instance is highly efficient for workloads requiring balanced compute and memory, such as software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference, though it lacks local storage.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-48 is an x86_64 server featuring 48 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 180 GB of RAM, and advanced AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets, optimized for balanced general-purpose workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-48 is a general-purpose server with 48 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 180 GB RAM, and no local storage.
tagline: A balanced 48-vCPU general-purpose instance featuring Intel Xeon processors with advanced matrix extensions for accelerated compute and memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor at 2.3 GHz
180.0 GB of system memory providing 3.75 GB per core
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated cryptography and matrix operations
Strong performance in static web serving, Redis operations, and software compilation
No bundled local storage or integrated GPU accelerators
Best suited for database operations, web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference
